source: Existing PMAD/PrimKD logit-only 5-run evidence in final daima docs and miou_list
hypothesis: The repeat-backed PMAD logit-only w=0.15/T=4 setting remains the strongest current positive KD direction and may produce a high-tail run approaching the 0.53 goal while adding one more stability sample.
proposed_change_scope: No model-structure change. Use existing dformerv2_primkd_logit_only with kd_weight=0.15, kd_temperature=4.0, frozen geometry-primary teacher, and save_student_only. Only keep train.py UTF-8/TQDM callback process fix.
expected_signal: Based on prior 5-run mean 0.520795 and best 0.524028, expected best val/mIoU is 0.514-0.525; success only if a high-tail run reaches >=0.53.
risk: This is a repeat rather than a new mechanism, so decision value is high mainly for goal chasing and stability estimation; prior distribution had not reached 0.53.
forbidden_change_check: No dataset split, eval metric, mIoU calculation, validation/test loader, augmentation, optimizer, scheduler, batch size, max_epochs, lr, num_workers, early_stop_patience, backbone, encoder, checkpoint artifact, dataset, pretrained weight, or TensorBoard event log change.
evidence_or_reference: PMAD logit-only w0.15/T4 prior five-run mean best 0.520795, std 0.003799, best single 0.524028; teacher checkpoint dformerv2_geometry_primary_teacher-epoch=37-val_mIoU=0.5168.pt. R010 retry1 completed best 0.527469 at epoch 49.
notes: Distinct from failed R001/R003 filtering and R009 feature hint. One hypothesis only: repeat the best existing logit-only KD setting as run06. The first run06 launch failed before validation with forrtl window-CLOSE; completed evidence is run06_retry1.

source: R010 partial-positive TensorBoard/checkpoint evidence plus existing PMAD/PrimKD logit-only repeat history
hypothesis: A second fixed-recipe PMAD logit-only w=0.15/T=4 repeat can test whether the R010 high-tail result is reproducible and may reach the >=0.53 target without changing model structure or training recipe.
proposed_change_scope: No code or model-structure change. Use existing dformerv2_primkd_logit_only with kd_weight=0.15, kd_temperature=4.0, frozen geometry-primary teacher, and save_student_only as run07.
expected_signal: R010 achieved 0.527469 and the updated six-run PMAD mean is 0.521907. Expected best val/mIoU is 0.517-0.528; success only with real best val/mIoU >=0.53.
risk: Repeat experiments can consume GPU time without adding mechanism novelty; if run07 falls below 0.526, avoid further blind repeats and return to a distinct mechanism.
forbidden_change_check: No dataset split, eval metric, mIoU calculation, validation/test loader, augmentation, optimizer, scheduler, batch size, max_epochs, lr, num_workers, early_stop_patience, backbone, encoder, checkpoint artifact, dataset, pretrained weight, or TensorBoard event log change.
evidence_or_reference: R010 dformerv2_primkd_logit_only_w015_t4_run06_retry1: best val/mIoU 0.527469 at epoch 49 from TensorBoard and checkpoint evidence; R012 completed with best val/mIoU 0.516967 at epoch 43 and exit code 0.
notes: One hypothesis only: repeat the same logit-only KD setting as run07. Distinct from R001/R003 selectors and R009 feature hint. No new model code was changed. Result argues against more blind PMAD w0.15/T4 repeats.

source: SegFormer All-MLP decoder paper/code and DFormer official local LMLPDecoder implementation
hypothesis: Replacing SimpleFPN's top-down additive decoder with a DFormer/SegFormer-style c2-c4 LMLP head may better aggregate fused transformer multi-scale features while avoiding c1 high-resolution noise.
proposed_change_scope: Add an independent LMLP decoder/head and model name dformerv2_lmlp_decoder. Use c2/c3/c4 MLP projections, upsample to c2, concatenate, 1x1 fuse+BN+ReLU+dropout+classifier, then upsample logits to input size. Do not change encoder, backbone, GatedFusion, data, eval, optimizer, scheduler, or fixed recipe.
expected_signal: Given prior decoder-swap negatives, expected best val/mIoU is 0.513-0.524. A result >=0.526 would be valuable; >=0.53 would meet the goal.
risk: Decoder/head swaps have repeatedly underperformed in this project. LMLP may lose c1 detail and could be below baseline, but it gives a clean answer about whether SimpleFPN top-down addition is the bottleneck.
forbidden_change_check: No dataset split, eval metric, mIoU calculation, validation/test loader, augmentation, optimizer, scheduler, batch size, max_epochs, lr, num_workers, early_stop_patience, backbone, encoder, checkpoint artifact, dataset, pretrained weight, or TensorBoard event log change.
evidence_or_reference: Paper: SegFormer: Simple and Efficient Design for Semantic Segmentation with Transformers, https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.15203; official repo: https://github.com/NVlabs/SegFormer; RGB-D DFormer repo: https://github.com/VCIP-RGBD/DFormer; local core: ref_codes/DFormer/models/decoders/LMLPDecoder.py and MLPDecoder.py. R013 completed best val/mIoU 0.517981 at epoch 41.
notes: Distinct from R002 frequency-aware FPN, R006 LightHam/NMF, R011 Lovasz loss, and PMAD/TGGA fusion routes. One hypothesis only: decoder/head aggregation. Result is only +0.000584 over baseline mean and has a 0.027750 best-to-last drop, so do not repeat unchanged.
